prompt: Implement a complete Byte-Pair Encoding (BPE) tokenizer in Python from scratch. The implementation must include: a Tokenizer class with methods train(corpus: list[str], vocab_size: int), encode(text: str) -> list[int], decode(ids: list[int]) -> str, save(path: str), and load(path: str). The train method must: initialize vocab with all unique bytes (256 tokens), count all adjacent byte pairs in the corpus, iteratively merge the most frequent pair, repeat until vocab_size is reached, store merges as an ordered list of (pair, new_token) tuples. The encode method must apply learned merges in order. The decode method must reverse the encoding. save/load must use JSON. Also implement get_stats(ids) -> dict[tuple, int] and merge(ids, pair, new_id) -> list[int] as standalone functions. Use only the standard library. Include a demo at the bottom that trains on a list of sample strings and encodes/decodes a test sentence. No comments, code only.

Implement a thread-safe LRU cache with TTL expiry in Python. Requirements: LRUCache[K, V] generic class using typing.Generic[K, V]; constructor takes capacity: int and ttl_seconds: float; get(key: K) -> V | None returns None if missing or expired; put(key: K, value: V) -> None evicts LRU when over capacity; delete(key: K) -> bool; clear() -> None; size() -> int; items() -> list[tuple[K, V]] returns non-expired items; a background thread must run every ttl_seconds/4 seconds to purge expired entries and must be a daemon thread so it stops when the process exits; use threading.Lock for thread safety; use collections.OrderedDict for O(1) LRU operations; entry expiry is based on insertion time not access time; implement __len__, __contains__, __repr__; include a comprehensive test suite in __main__ that tests all methods, expiry behavior, thread safety with 10 concurrent readers/writers, and capacity eviction. No comments, no docstrings, code only.

Implement an async data pipeline framework in Python using asyncio. The framework must have: a Stage base class with abstract method process(item: Any) -> Any; a Pipeline class that chains multiple stages; a BufferedStage that wraps a Stage with an asyncio.Queue of configurable size for backpressure; a BatchStage that accumulates items and processes them in batches of configurable size and maximum wait time; a FilterStage that drops items not matching a predicate; a MapStage that applies a transformation function; a FanoutStage that sends each item to multiple downstream stages concurrently; a MergeStage that merges multiple input queues into one output queue using asyncio.gather; a RateLimiterStage that enforces a maximum throughput in items per second using a token bucket algorithm; a MetricsStage that tracks items_in, items_out, errors, and latency_p50/p95/p99 and exposes them as a dict via get_metrics(). Include an example pipeline that reads integers from an async generator, filters even numbers, multiplies by 3, batches into groups of 5, and prints each batch. No comments, code only.
